evals.prompt: Turn this PRD for adding saved payment methods into issues agents can pick up independently.
evals.expected_output: The agent breaks the PRD into tracer-bullet vertical slices, maximizes same-wave parallelism, distinguishes blockers from coordination risks, and publishes without asking for approval.
assertions: Creates thin vertical slices that cross integration layers end to end.
Avoids horizontal layer-only issues.
Shows title, wave, blocked-by relationships, edge type, parallel-safe siblings, coordination risks, and user stories covered for each slice.
Avoids serializing unrelated slices and distinguishes real blockers from coordination risks.
Treats tracking issues, shared files, dirty worktrees, changelog edits, and nearby tests as coordination risks unless they are concrete blockers.
Avoids creating a global setup or tracking issue that blocks all implementation work.
Keeps behavior-specific regression tests inside the relevant behavior slices unless a shared corpus is a true prerequisite.
Moves every issue into the earliest safe execution wave before publishing.
Publishes without asking the user for approval.

evals.prompt: Use the approved breakdown and publish the issues to our tracker.
evals.expected_output: The agent publishes GitHub issues with gh CLI or the helper script, creates missing labels, publishes blockers first, preserves same-wave parallelism, reports launch batches, and does not close or modify the parent issue.
assertions: Confirms gh auth status and destination repository before publishing to GitHub.
Uses scripts/publish_github_issues.py or equivalent gh issue create commands.
Creates missing labels before creating issues.
Publishes blockers before dependent issues.
Publishes same-wave issues in stable order while preserving parallel-safe grouping.
Applies the ready-for-agent triage label unless instructed otherwise.
References real blocking issue identifiers after creation.
Includes execution wave and parallelism sections in issue bodies.
Reports created GitHub issue URLs.
Reports same-wave launch batches for separate agents/worktrees when the user intends agent execution.
Does not close or modify the parent issue.
